title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. Oliver meets with Hercule Poirot to discuss the murder of Joyce, a boastful and self-important girl found dead in a library. Mrs. Oliver explains that during the party hosted by Roya Delek, which was attended by approximately twenty-five to thirty people including various mothers, school teachers, and young people, Joyce had made a startling claim. Earlier that day, while preparing for the party, Joyce insisted she had witnessed a murder a long time ago, though she had not realized it was a murder until she suddenly reflected on it. Although the adults and children mocked her and did not believe her, Mrs. Oliver now suspects Joyce was telling the truth and was killed because she was a witness.

Poirot observes that the murder was a cruel, well-timed act involving holding the victim's head under a bucket of water. He theorizes that someone who heard Joyce's claims earlier that day might have known the identity of the original killer and murdered Joyce to prevent her from revealing the truth. Poirot suggests the killer might be someone protecting a family member.

Following the discussion, Poirot travels to a house called "The Crowned One" on a hilltop. There, he encounters Superintendent Spence, who is tending to his garden. Poirot reveals that he found Spence's location through a Christmas card the Superintendent had sent. The two men share a past connection involving a previous murder case.
